name: San Diego Financial Literacy Center
description_short: San Diego Financial Literacy Center provides free financial education seminars, workshops, and one-on-one consultations to San Diego County residents, with specialized programs for youth, military families, and low-to-moderate income individuals.
description_long:
The San Diego Financial Literacy Center (SDFLC) was founded in late 2013 and opened to the public on February 26, 2014, as a community outreach program integrated into DebtWave Credit Counseling, Inc., a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. The organization emerged from DebtWave's 2001 founding mission to address widespread budgetary challenges and financial education gaps in San Diego County. The SDFLC rebranded DebtWave's existing community outreach efforts to focus comprehensively on overall financial health across the region.

The SDFLC offers three core free programs: Boost For Our Heroes (financial assistance and education for military members and families), Smart With Your Money or S.W.Y.M (complimentary financial literacy seminars for individuals, families, and organizations), and Wave Of The Future (financial education for high school and college students covering topics like debt avoidance and proper credit use). Beyond these structured programs, they provide complimentary educational seminars and workshops customized to constituent needs, plus confidential one-on-one financial consultations and budget analysis. Educational content is also delivered through informational videos available on their website.

The SDFLC distinguishes itself through its collaborative approach, working with community, corporate, military, and educational partners throughout San Diego County to deliver non-duplicative resources. Their focus specifically targets three underserved populations: youth (K-12 and college), active/transitioning/veteran military members, and low-to-moderate income persons. The organization actively tracks outcomes through surveys, quarterly contacts, and regular touchpoints to ensure curriculum effectiveness, and relies on volunteer support alongside dedicated staff.

As a completely free nonprofit resource, the SDFLC has genuine value for targeted populations, particularly military families and students. However, the organization's reach is limited to San Diego County, and their website provides minimal detail about counselor credentials, specific consultation availability, or wait times. The organization's outcomes tracking focuses on contact frequency rather than measurable financial improvements, and their blog content suggests educational rather than crisis intervention capacity.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Completely free services across all programs—no hidden fees or charges
Three specialized programs addressing distinct populations: military families, general public, and students
One-on-one confidential financial consultations and budget analysis available in addition to group seminars
Part of established 501(c)(3) nonprofit DebtWave with 20+ years operational history (since 2001)
Multiple physical locations across San Diego County (Escondido headquarters, National City, and Escondido Military & Veterans Resource Center)
Offers customized, non-duplicative educational workshops tailored to organizational and individual needs
Active volunteer program and community/corporate partnerships extend organizational reach
cons: Limited to San Diego County residents only—no services for out-of-area consumers
Website lacks information about counselor certifications, credentials, or NFCC affiliation status despite being categorized as free-help
No details provided about consultation wait times, availability, or how to schedule one-on-one sessions
Outcomes tracking focuses on contact frequency and surveys rather than demonstrable financial improvement metrics
Educational focus appears primarily preventative rather than crisis intervention for those in immediate financial distress
best_for: Active military members, veterans, and military families seeking financial education and assistance programs
High school and college students learning foundational personal finance and credit management skills
San Diego County low-to-moderate income individuals wanting free budget counseling and financial planning workshops
Organizations and employers in San Diego County seeking customized financial literacy seminars for employees or members
diagnosis: The SDFLC is best for San Diego County residents—particularly military families, students, and low-income individuals—seeking free financial education and guidance from an established nonprofit. The main caveat is that their services are location-restricted to San Diego County, counselor credentials are not publicly disclosed, and their strength is in preventative education rather than crisis debt intervention.
services: Boost For Our Heroes—financial assistance and education program for military members and families
Smart With Your Money (S.W.Y.M)—complimentary financial literacy seminars for individuals and organizations
Wave Of The Future—financial education for high school and college students on debt and credit topics
Custom budget analysis and financial planning
Customized educational seminars and workshops for organizations
Quarterly follow-up contacts and outcome tracking for program participants
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 9325 Sky Park Ct suite 260, San Diego, CA 92123
